message:
fix(cli): use naive UTC datetimes in doctor commands for consistency

The FileReservation model stores timestamps as naive UTC datetimes
(via _utcnow_naive()). The doctor check and repair commands were
incorrectly using datetime.now(timezone.utc) which creates aware
datetimes. This inconsistency could cause comparison issues when
querying expired reservations.

Fixed by using .replace(tzinfo=None) to get naive UTC datetimes,
matching the pattern used throughout the rest of the codebase
(e.g., app.py uses _naive_utc() for FileReservation comparisons).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

                     ))
 
             # Check 5: Expired file reservations
-            now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
+            # Use naive UTC datetime for consistency with how FileReservation stores timestamps
+            now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None)
             expired_query = select(func.count()).select_from(FileReservation).where(
                 and_(
                     cast(ColumnElement[bool], cast(Any, FileReservation.released_ts).is_(None)),
